zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Ross於1435年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可內特萊斯亞德時，她的父親Sir John Ross 1st Lord Of Halkhead22歲，母親Marjory Mure of Caldwell20歲。她和Sir Thomas Sempill of Eliotstoun至少有1個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1495年11月13日在英國蘇格蘭雷福福爾享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Ross于1435年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可內特萊斯亞德时，她的父亲Sir John Ross 1st Lord Of Halkhead22岁，母亲Marjory Mure of Caldwell20岁。她和Sir Thomas Sempill of Eliotstoun至少有1个儿子和3个女儿。她去世于1495年11月13日在英国苏格兰雷福福爾享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Ross
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

variants.name: Ruskin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Tauba, Uzi.
English: from the Middle English personal name Rosce (see Ross 4) + the diminutive suffix -kin.
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish (Ashkenazic) surname, for example   Raskin  .

variants.name: Ros
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.
Swedish: ornamental name from ros ‘rose’.

variants.name: Rose
meanings: English, Scottish, French, Walloon, Danish, and German: from the name of the flower, Middle English, Old French, Middle High German rose (from Latin rosa), in various applications. In part, it is a topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house bearing the sign of the rose. It is also found, especially in Europe, as a nickname for a man with a ‘rosy’ complexion (compare 4 below). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Rózsa (see Rozsa ), Slovak Róža and Czech Roza . Compare 6 below and French Larose 2.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Rohese, Roese, later Rose, Royse (ancient Germanic Hrodohaidis, Rothaid, composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + haid(is) ‘kind, sort’).
English and Scottish: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rossell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Ros(c)el, a diminutive of Rosce (see Ross ). Compare the double diminutive Roscelin, with which Ro(s)cel or Ros(s)el was probably freely interchangeable.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rosel in Calvados.
English: variant of   Russell  , with which the name in 1 above was easily confused.

variants.name: Rusling
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Rocelin (Old French Roscelin, Rocelin, ancient Germanic Ruozelin), a double diminutive of Rozo (see Ross ).

variants.name: Rohs
meanings: German: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rosse
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Joris.
German: topographic name from Middle High German rœsse ‘kiln’, in particular one used for drying hemp and flax or for burning lime.
English and Scottish (Lanarkshire): variant of   Ross  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Ross was born in 1435, in Nettlestead, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir John Ross 1st Lord Of Halkhead, was 22 and her mother, Marjory Mure of Caldwell, was 20. She had at least 1 son and 3 daughters with Sir Thomas Sempill of Eliotstoun. She died on 13 November 1495, in Renfrewshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Ross est née en 1435, à Nettlestead, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John Ross 1st Lord Of Halkhead, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Marjory Mure of Caldwell, avait 20 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles avec Sir Thomas Sempill of Eliotstoun. Elle est décédée le 13 novembre 1495, à Renfrewshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Ross 1435 in Nettlestead, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Ross 1st Lord Of Halkhead 22 und ihre Mutter Marjory Mure of Caldwell 20. Sie hatte mit Sir Thomas Sempill of Eliotstoun mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 13. November 1495 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Renfrewshire, Schottland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Ross è nata il nel 1435 nel Nettlestead, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir John Ross 1st Lord Of Halkhead, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Marjory Mure of Caldwell, aveva 20 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie con Sir Thomas Sempill of Eliotstoun. Morì il 13 novembre 1495, nel Renfrewshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Rossが1435年にイギリスイギリススフォルクネットレステアドで生まれた時，父Sir John Ross 1st Lord Of Halkheadは22歳，母Marjory Mure of Caldwellは20歳であった。彼女はSir Thomas Sempill of Eliotstounとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1495年11月13日にイギリススコットランドレンフリューシュアイアで60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Ross(이)가 1435년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 네틀레스테아드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Ross 1st Lord Of Halkhead(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marjory Mure of Caldwell(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 Sir Thomas Sempill of Eliotstoun(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1495년 11월 13일 영국 스코틀랜드 렌프레w실르에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Ross nasceu em 1435, em Nettlestead, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir John Ross 1st Lord Of Halkhead, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Marjory Mure of Caldwell, tinha 20 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas com Sir Thomas Sempill of Eliotstoun. Ela faleceu em 13 de novembro de 1495, em Renfrewshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 60 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Ross родилась в 1435 г. в местности неттлестид, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir John Ross 1st Lord Of Halkhead было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Marjory Mure of Caldwell, 20. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери от Sir Thomas Sempill of Eliotstoun. Она умерла 13 ноября 1495 г. в местности ренфрюшир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 60 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Ross nació en 1435, en Nettlestead, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir John Ross 1st Lord Of Halkhead, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Marjory Mure of Caldwell, tenía 20 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas con Sir Thomas Sempill of Eliotstoun. Murió el 13 de noviembre de 1495, en Renfrewshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 60 años.
